LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER CHARLES ROBERT GORTON'S MOTION TO DEEM PETITION OF WRIT OF MANDATE AS TIMELY. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OFF CALENDAR, PROOF OF SERVICE IS DEFECTIVE BECAUSE IT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 1013A(1). A NON-PARTY NEEDS TO SERVE ANY MOTION. IN ADDITION, THIS MOTION IS UNNECESSARY BECAUSE WHETHER THE PETITION WAS TIMELY FILED CAN BE RAISED AT THE HEARING ON THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHARLES GORTON'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THERE IS AMPLE EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S DECISION THAT PLAINTIFF VIOLATED THE CONTRACTOR'S LAW BY HAVING BEEN CONVICTED OF 37 KNOWN FELONY COUNTS OF CHILD MOLESTATION AND THE FELONY COUNTS ARE SUBSTANTIALLY RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S QUALIFICATION, FUNCTIONS AND DUTIES AS A CONTRACTOR. LICENSED CONTRACTORS PERFORM THEIR WORK IN CONSUMER'S HOMES OR BUSINESS; OFTEN THERE ARE FAMILY MEMBERS PRESENT, INCLUDING CHILDREN. MS. LEDAKIS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN CSR #5220
OPPOSITION AND OBJECTIONS TO THE COURT'S AND ATTORNEY GENERAL'S PROPOSED ORDER JUDGMENT DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE FILED BY PLAINTIFF GORTON, CHARLES ROBERT T-43446

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition And Petition For Rehearing
Off Calendar Apr-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CHARLES GORTON'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. Appearances: Sherry Ledakis of the Attorney General's Office (858.794.0990) appearing via Court Call for respondent and opposing party; Charles Gorton, petitioner and moving party appearing telephonically. Argued and the court ruled as follows: denied. The court finds there is ample evidence to support the administrative law judge's decision that plaintiff violated the contractor's law by having been convicted of 37 known felony counts of child molestation and the felony counts are substantially related to plaintiff's qualification, functions and duties as a contractor. Licensed contractors perform their work in consumer's homes or business; often there are family members present, including children. Ms. Ledakis to submit a proposed form of order. (302/REQ) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Reporter: Robert Balian CSR #5220

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Petition Of Writ Of Mandate As Timely
Off Calendar Aug-06-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER CHARLES ROBERT GORTON'S MOTION TO DEEM PETITION OF WRIT OF MANDATE AS TIMELY.

APPEARANCES: Charles Robert Gorton, petitioner & moving party in propria persona (appearing telephonically); no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR, PROOF OF SERVICE IS DEFECTIVE BECAUSE IT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 1013a(1). A NON-PARTY NEEDS TO SERVE ANY MOTION. IN ADDITION, THIS MOTION IS UNNECESSARY BECAUSE WHETHER THE PETITION WAS TIMELY FILED CAN BE RAISED AT THE HEARING ON THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
